rationale: The citing paper discusses MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) with specific findings (affective cues being more sales-effective than informational/heuristic cues), not just as a name in a grouped list. It includes the study in a comparative prior-literature table and uses it to position what is new in the current paper (adding online search outcomes and product-positioning heterogeneity). The cited work also reappears in interpretation/limitations around content effects over time, indicating an ongoing conceptual role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on MacInnis et al. (2002) as prior evidence on informational versus affective ad-content effects on sales, then extends that line by modeling both online search and sales together and by testing moderation by price and quality in the auto market.
